item.dc:creator: MovieCaveDave

item.title: A Russian soldier handed over a tank to Ukrainian soldiers for a reward
item.description: "Misha" called us a few days ago. We passed information about him to the Central Intelligence Agency of Ukraine. He was assigned a place to approach. He drove up there in a tank. From a drone they were convinced that he and it isn't an ambush. After that special forces detained him. It turned out that he was left alone from the tank crew, the rest fled home. He saw no point in fighting. He could not return home because the commander said he would kill and write off the battle losses. Misha said that there was almost nothing left, the command of the troops was chaotic and practically absent. The demoralization is enormous. What about Misha? Misha received comfortable conditions. He will also receive $ 10,000 after the end of the war and the opportunity to apply for Ukrainian citizenship. Until the end of the war, he will live in comfortable conditions with TV, telephone, kitchen and shower.
item.dc:creator: maksmixalets365

item.title: Retired US Army General on why Russia is taking so many casualties in Ukraine
item.description:
Gen. Hertling is the former commanding officer of the First Armored Division and also served for a period as overall commander of US Army forces in Europe.
On March 21, Komsomolskaya Pravda, a pro-Kremlin tabloid, published figures on their website saying 9,861 Russian soldiers have died in Ukraine and 16,153 have been injured.
This was ostensibly their way of pushing back against Ukrainian claims of Russian losses: "See? It's not actually that bad." The figures were quickly removed. If it wasn't clear, if these are the numbers Russia admits to, the real numbers are certainly worse.
As a comparison: 15,000 Russian troops died in 10 years in Afghanistan in the 70's and 80's. US deaths over 20 years in Afghanistan and Iraq were about 6000.
https://twitter.com/MarkHertling
item.dc:creator: JohnnyGossamer

item.title: Anti-Vaxxer explains Freedom of Speech in the most ironic way possible.
item.description: After Searching , his name is Randy Ireland from NYC and he's a proud boy, he gets called up on a stage to give a speech on January 6 prosecutions and Capitol rally He has a video they put up a banner saying "free political prisoners" or similar about Jan 6th and he gets called up on a stage to give a speech. here is the images and video : https://tllg.me/VBoQX

item.title: Healthcare Worker Memes posted in timely night-shift fashion
item.description:
AMA- signing out against medical advice.
I'm looking at you, Geraldine.
GCS- Glasgow Coma Scale. It is an assessment for a person's level of consciousness, scored 3-15 based on three types of stimuli (eye-opening, verbal response, and motor response). A score of 16/15 probably means you can smell colors
Sundowning- a phenomenon in which elderly people become drastically confused at night. They may be pleasant and oriented during the day but at night they are confused, disoriented, and impulsive. Like gremlins!
And nurses be like... damn, this fucker again
Still stings.
An ideal blood pressure is 120/80. A low blood pressure causes concern that blood is not circulating the body enough to provide sustenance for tissue. MAP (mean arterial pressure) is the diastolic (80) x2 plus the systolic (120) and divided by 3, equaling 93 The MAP needs to be greater than 65 for the body to be perfused adequately. Someone with a pressure of 79/58 would likely end up on pressors (medications to raise blood pressure like norepinephrine)
A lot of unprocessed trauma.
Damnit Geraldine!!
Love it when those horrible patients leave and stop wasting resources.
Kick rocks. Last week I put an 18g in the foot of a drug addict writhing around in a stretcher vomiting.
Foreign body removal ;) this one's for you emergency, colorectal and general surgery
Cat Tax My favorite cat Stella on my favorite blanket.
item.dc:creator: MEGALOAF
